diary:
Woke up to perfect Sunday light filtering through my Belltown place. Did a lazy bike loop through the neighborhood, hit up Belltown Thrift and scored this sick vintage Sonic Youth tee for like eight bucks—still riding that high. Coffee at Fuel in Fremont felt necessary; I just sat there for almost an hour watching the Sunday crowd, letting my brain decompress before the week hits.

Wandered Green Lake afterward. The water was packed with boats and paddleboarders, but I stayed on the path, just walking and thinking. Grabbed a slice at Serious Pie and ate outside in the sun. Came home for chess puzzles—needed that quiet focus. Then texted Marcus and we met up at Discovery Park for a walk. Nothing fancy, just talking through stuff, watching the Sound. Grocery run on the way back, threw together a decent dinner with some music playing. The kind of Sunday that feels like a reset button.
thought_process: The weather was too good to waste, so I leaned into the slow, wandering energy of it all. No pressure today—no studio appointments, no deadlines. I think I needed that after a busy week of client work. The thrift store run was about feeding that creative part of me that likes finding random treasures; same with the coffee meditation at Fuel. I saw all those boats on Lake Union and felt content just observing from the park instead of joining in. By evening I was craving connection, so Marcus's company grounded me. The whole day felt like I was choosing presence over productivity, and that shift in mindset made everything feel lighter.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
